sentences.sentence: The Industrial Revolution began in Britain in the mid-1700s and by the 1830s and 1840s had spread to many other parts of the world, including the United States.
sentences.translation: 工业革命于18世纪中期在英国开始，到19世纪30年代和40年代已经传播到世界许多其他地区，包括美国。

structure.text: The Industrial Revolution
began in Britain in the mid-1700s
and by the 1830s and 1840s had spread to many other parts of the world
including the United States

difficult_points: began 和 had spread 共用主语 The Industrial Revolution，表示先开始、后扩散的时间推进。
by the 1830s and 1840s 表示“到……时候为止”，常与完成时连用。
including the United States 是补充说明 many other parts of the world 的范围。

expressions.text: many other parts of the world

sentences.sentence: In Britain, it was a period when a largely rural, agrarian* society was transformed into an industrialised, urban one.
sentences.translation: 在英国，这是一段一个以农村和农业为主的社会转变为工业化、城市化社会的时期。

structure.text: In Britain
it was a period
when a largely rural, agrarian* society was transformed into an industrialised, urban one
was transformed into an industrialised, urban one

difficult_points: it 指代前一句提到的 Industrial Revolution，而不是 Britain。
when 引导定语从句修饰 a period，说明这一时期发生了什么变化。
was transformed into 是被动结构，强调社会被改变为新的形态。

expressions.meaning_zh: 一个以农村为主的社会

sentences.sentence: Goods that had once been crafted by hand started to be produced in mass quantities by machines in factories, thanks to the invention of steam power and the introduction of new machines and manufacturing techniques in textiles, iron-making and other industries.
sentences.translation: 由于蒸汽动力的发明以及纺织、炼铁和其他行业中新机器和制造技术的引入，曾经由手工制作的商品开始在工厂里由机器大批量生产。

structure.text: Goods that had once been crafted by hand
that had once been crafted by hand
started to be produced in mass quantities by machines in factories
thanks to the invention of steam power and the introduction of new machines and manufacturing techniques
in textiles, iron-making and other industries

difficult_points: that had once been crafted by hand 是定语从句，说明 goods 过去的生产方式。
started to be produced 中的不定式是被动形式，表示商品开始被生产。
thanks to 后面的两个并列名词短语解释大规模机器生产出现的原因。

sentences.sentence: The foundations of the Industrial Revolution date back to the early 1700s, when the English inventor Thomas Newcomen designed the first modern steam engine.
sentences.translation: 工业革命的基础可以追溯到18世纪初，当时英国发明家托马斯·纽科门设计出了第一台现代蒸汽机。

structure.text: The foundations of the Industrial Revolution
date back to the early 1700s
when the English inventor Thomas Newcomen designed the first modern steam engine
the English inventor Thomas Newcomen

difficult_points: date back to 表示“追溯到”，不是普通的“约会”含义。
when 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 early 1700s 这一时间点。
Thomas Newcomen 与 the English inventor 是同位关系。

sentences.sentence: Called the "atmospheric steam engine", Newcomen's invention was originally used to power machines that pumped water out of mines.
sentences.translation: 纽科门的发明被称为“常压蒸汽机”，最初用于驱动把水从矿井中抽出的机器。

structure.text: Called the "atmospheric steam engine"
Newcomen's invention
was originally used to power machines
that pumped water out of mines

difficult_points: Called the atmospheric steam engine 是过去分词短语，补充说明该发明的名称。
was originally used to power 是被动结构，说明最初用途。
that pumped water out of mines 修饰 machines，而不是 invention。

sentences.sentence: In the 1760s, the Scottish engineer James Watt started to adapt one of Newcomen's models, and succeeded in making it far more efficient.
sentences.translation: 18世纪60年代，苏格兰工程师詹姆斯·瓦特开始改造纽科门的一台机器，并成功使其效率大大提高。

structure.text: In the 1760s
the Scottish engineer James Watt
started to adapt one of Newcomen's models
and succeeded in making it far more efficient
making it far more efficient

difficult_points: James Watt 与 the Scottish engineer 构成同位说明。
started 和 succeeded 是并列谓语，描述瓦特连续完成的两个动作。
it 指代 one of Newcomen's models，far more efficient 表示效率提升幅度很大。

sentences.sentence: Watt later worked with the English manufacturer Matthew Boulton to invent a new steam engine driven by both the forward and backward strokes of the piston, while the gear mechanism it was connected to produced rotary motion.
sentences.translation: 瓦特后来与英国制造商马修·博尔顿合作，发明了一种由活塞前后冲程共同驱动的新型蒸汽机，而与它相连的齿轮机构则产生旋转运动。

structure.text: Watt
worked with the English manufacturer Matthew Boulton to invent a new steam engine
driven by both the forward and backward strokes of the piston
while the gear mechanism it was connected to produced rotary motion
it was connected to

difficult_points: to invent 表示 worked with 的目的，即合作是为了发明新蒸汽机。
driven by 是过去分词短语，修饰 a new steam engine。
while 引导对比性的并列情况，it was connected to 省略了关系代词，修饰 gear mechanism。

sentences.sentence: It was a key innovation that would allow steam power to spread across British industries.
sentences.translation: 这是一项关键创新，它将使蒸汽动力得以在英国各行业中推广。

structure.text: It was a key innovation
that would allow steam power to spread across British industries
allow steam power to spread across British industries

difficult_points: It 指代前一句瓦特和博尔顿的新蒸汽机设计。
that 引导定语从句，说明 key innovation 的作用。
would allow 表示从当时视角看后续将产生的结果。

sentences.sentence: The demand for coal, which was a relatively cheap energy source, grew rapidly during the Industrial Revolution, as it was needed to run not only the factories used to produce manufactured goods, but also steam-powered transportation.
sentences.translation: 煤炭是一种相对廉价的能源；由于它不仅需要用于运转生产制成品的工厂，也需要用于蒸汽动力交通，对煤炭的需求在工业革命期间迅速增长。

structure.text: The demand for coal
which was a relatively cheap energy source
grew rapidly during the Industrial Revolution
as it was needed to run not only the factories used to produce manufactured goods, but also steam-powered transportation
not only the factories used to produce manufactured goods, but also steam-powered transportation

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 coal 的能源属性。
as 引导原因状语从句，解释煤炭需求快速增长的原因。
not only...but also... 连接两个被 run 支配的对象。

sentences.sentence: In the early 1800s, the English engineer Richard Trevithick built a steam-powered locomotive, and by 1830 goods and passengers were being transported between the industrial centres of Manchester and Liverpool.
sentences.translation: 19世纪初，英国工程师理查德·特里维西克制造了一台蒸汽动力机车，到1830年，货物和乘客已经在曼彻斯特和利物浦这两个工业中心之间被运输。

structure.text: In the early 1800s
the English engineer Richard Trevithick
built a steam-powered locomotive
and by 1830 goods and passengers were being transported between the industrial centres of Manchester and Liverpool
were being transported

difficult_points: 句子用 and 连接两个时间阶段：先有蒸汽机车，后有客货运输。
were being transported 是过去进行时的被动语态，强调1830年前后的运输正在进行。
between the industrial centres of Manchester and Liverpool 表示运输发生在两个工业中心之间。

sentences.sentence: In addition, steam-powered boats and ships were widely used to carry goods along Britain's canals as well as across the Atlantic.
sentences.translation: 此外，蒸汽动力的船只也被广泛用于沿英国运河以及横跨大西洋运输货物。

structure.text: In addition
steam-powered boats and ships
were widely used to carry goods
along Britain's canals as well as across the Atlantic

difficult_points: In addition 表示在铁路运输之外继续补充水上运输。
were widely used 是被动结构，强调蒸汽船的用途范围很广。
as well as 连接 along Britain's canals 和 across the Atlantic 两个运输路径。

sentences.sentence: Britain had produced textiles like wool, linen and cotton for hundreds of years, but prior to the Industrial Revolution, the British textile business was a true "cottage industry", with the work performed in small workshops or even homes by individual spinners, weavers and dyers.
sentences.translation: 英国生产羊毛、亚麻和棉花等纺织品已有数百年，但在工业革命之前，英国纺织业是真正的“家庭手工业”，工作由个体纺纱工、织布工和染工在小作坊甚至家中完成。

structure.text: Britain had produced textiles like wool, linen and cotton for hundreds of years
but prior to the Industrial Revolution, the British textile business was a true "cottage industry"
prior to the Industrial Revolution
with the work performed in small workshops or even homes by individual spinners, weavers and dyers
performed in small workshops or even homes

difficult_points: had produced 是过去完成时，强调工业革命之前已经持续了数百年的生产历史。
but 引出转折：虽然生产历史悠久，但生产组织形式仍是家庭手工业。
with the work performed... 是 with 复合结构，说明工作的完成地点和执行者。

sentences.sentence: Starting in the mid-1700s, innovations like the spinning jenny and the power loom made weaving cloth and spinning yarn and thread much easier.
sentences.translation: 从18世纪中期开始，珍妮纺纱机和动力织布机等创新让织布以及纺纱、纺线变得容易得多。

structure.text: Starting in the mid-1700s
innovations like the spinning jenny and the power loom
made weaving cloth and spinning yarn and thread much easier
weaving cloth and spinning yarn and thread
much easier

difficult_points: Starting in the mid-1700s 是现在分词短语，给出创新出现的起始时间。
made 后接“宾语 + 宾语补足语”，表示让某事变得更容易。
weaving 和 spinning 是动名词，整体作 made 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: With these machines, relatively little labour was required to produce cloth, and the new, mechanised textile factories that opened around the country were quickly able to meet customer demand for cloth both at home and abroad.
sentences.translation: 有了这些机器，生产布匹所需的劳动力相对很少，而在全国各地开设的新型机械化纺织厂很快就能够满足国内外顾客对布匹的需求。

structure.text: With these machines
relatively little labour was required to produce cloth
and the new, mechanised textile factories that opened around the country were quickly able to meet customer demand for cloth both at home and abroad
that opened around the country
both at home and abroad

difficult_points: was required 是被动语态，说明生产布匹所需的劳动力数量。
that opened around the country 修饰 textile factories。
both at home and abroad 表示顾客需求来自国内和海外两个市场。

sentences.sentence: The British iron industry also underwent major change as it adopted new innovations.
sentences.translation: 随着英国铁工业采用新的创新技术，它也经历了重大变化。

structure.text: The British iron industry
also underwent major change
as it adopted new innovations

difficult_points: underwent major change 表示“经历重大变化”，主语是 industry。
as 引导时间兼原因状语从句，说明变化伴随新技术采用而发生。
it 指代 The British iron industry。

sentences.sentence: Chief among the new techniques was the smelting of iron ore with coke (a material made by heating coal) instead of the traditional charcoal.
sentences.translation: 这些新技术中最重要的是用焦炭（通过加热煤制成的一种材料）而不是传统木炭来冶炼铁矿石。

structure.text: Chief among the new techniques
was
the smelting of iron ore with coke (a material made by heating coal) instead of the traditional charcoal
a material made by heating coal
instead of the traditional charcoal

difficult_points: 句首 Chief among the new techniques 是表语前置，正常理解为“最主要的新技术是……”。
the smelting of iron ore with coke 是名词化结构，指“用焦炭冶炼铁矿石”。
括号中的 a material made by heating coal 解释 coke 的含义。

sentences.sentence: This method was cheaper and produced metals that were of a higher quality, enabling Britain's iron and steel production to expand in response to demand created by the Napoleonic Wars (1803–15) and the expansion of the railways from the 1830s.
sentences.translation: 这种方法成本更低，并且生产出的金属质量更高，从而使英国的钢铁产量能够扩大，以回应拿破仑战争（1803—1815年）以及19世纪30年代起铁路扩张所带来的需求。

structure.text: This method
was cheaper and produced metals
that were of a higher quality
enabling Britain's iron and steel production to expand
in response to demand created by the Napoleonic Wars (1803–15) and the expansion of the railways from the 1830s

difficult_points: was cheaper and produced metals 是并列谓语，说明这种方法的两个优势。
that were of a higher quality 修饰 metals，of a higher quality 等于 higher-quality。
enabling... 是结果状语，说明前面优势带来的影响。

sentences.sentence: The latter part of the Industrial Revolution also saw key advances in communication methods, as people increasingly saw the need to communicate efficiently over long distances.
sentences.translation: 工业革命后期，通信方式也取得了关键进展，因为人们越来越意识到需要在长距离范围内高效沟通。

structure.text: The latter part of the Industrial Revolution
also saw key advances in communication methods
as people increasingly saw the need to communicate efficiently over long distances
to communicate efficiently over long distances

difficult_points: saw 在这里不是“看见”，而是表示某一时期“见证、出现”了进展。
as 引导原因状语从句，解释通信进步的社会需求。
the need 后接不定式 to communicate，说明具体需要是什么。

sentences.sentence: In 1837, British inventors William Cooke and Charles Wheatstone patented the first commercial telegraphy system.
sentences.translation: 1837年，英国发明家威廉·库克和查尔斯·惠斯通为第一个商业电报系统申请了专利。

structure.text: British inventors William Cooke and Charles Wheatstone
patented the first commercial telegraphy system

difficult_points: William Cooke and Charles Wheatstone 是 British inventors 的同位说明。
patented 是动词，表示“获得专利、申请专利保护”。
the first commercial telegraphy system 中 commercial 限定该系统的实际商业用途。

expressions.meaning_zh: 为第一个商业系统申请专利

sentences.sentence: In the 1830s and 1840s, Samuel Morse and other inventors worked on their own versions in the United States.
sentences.translation: 在19世纪30年代和40年代，塞缪尔·莫尔斯和其他发明家在美国研发他们自己的版本。

structure.text: In the 1830s and 1840s
Samuel Morse and other inventors
worked on their own versions
in the United States

difficult_points: worked on 表示“从事、研发”，不是简单的“在……上工作”。
their own versions 指代他们自己的电报系统版本。
in the United States 说明研发地点，与前一句英国发明形成对照。

sentences.sentence: Cooke and Wheatstone's system was soon used for railway signalling in the UK.
sentences.translation: 库克和惠斯通的系统很快被用于英国的铁路信号传递。

structure.text: Cooke and Wheatstone's system
was soon used for railway signalling
in the UK

difficult_points: was soon used 是被动语态，说明系统很快获得实际应用。
for railway signalling 表示用途，即用于铁路信号传递。
Cooke and Wheatstone's 是并列所有格，指两人共同的系统。

sentences.sentence: As the speed of the new locomotives increased, it was essential to have a fast and effective means of avoiding collisions.
sentences.translation: 随着新型机车速度提高，拥有一种快速且有效的避免碰撞手段变得至关重要。

structure.text: As the speed of the new locomotives increased
it was essential
to have a fast and effective means of avoiding collisions
of avoiding collisions

difficult_points: As 引导时间状语从句，表示随着机车速度提高。
it 是形式主语，真正主语是不定式 to have...。
means of avoiding collisions 表示“避免碰撞的手段”，of 后接动名词。

expressions.text: a fast and effective means of

sentences.sentence: The impact of the Industrial Revolution on people's lives was immense.
sentences.translation: 工业革命对人们生活的影响是巨大的。

structure.text: The impact of the Industrial Revolution on people's lives
of the Industrial Revolution
on people's lives
was immense

difficult_points: 主语核心词是 impact，两个介词短语分别说明影响的来源和对象。
immense 表示程度很大，用来概括工业革命对生活的整体影响。
句子结构短，但 of 和 on 的修饰关系需要分清。

sentences.sentence: Although many people in Britain had begun moving to the cities from rural areas before the Industrial Revolution, this accelerated dramatically with industrialisation, as the rise of large factories turned smaller towns into major cities in just a few decades.
sentences.translation: 尽管英国许多人在工业革命前就已经开始从农村地区迁往城市，但随着工业化，这一过程急剧加速，因为大型工厂的兴起在短短几十年内把较小城镇变成了大城市。

structure.text: Although many people in Britain had begun moving to the cities from rural areas before the Industrial Revolution
this accelerated dramatically with industrialisation
as the rise of large factories turned smaller towns into major cities in just a few decades
turned smaller towns into major cities

difficult_points: Although 引导让步状语从句，说明人口迁移在工业革命前已开始。
this 指代前半句“从农村迁往城市”的趋势。
as 引导原因状语从句，解释城市化加速的原因。

sentences.sentence: This rapid urbanisation brought significant challenges, as overcrowded cities suffered from pollution and inadequate sanitation.
sentences.translation: 这种快速城市化带来了重大挑战，因为过度拥挤的城市遭受污染和卫生设施不足之苦。

structure.text: This rapid urbanisation
brought significant challenges
as overcrowded cities suffered from pollution and inadequate sanitation
suffered from pollution and inadequate sanitation

difficult_points: This rapid urbanisation 指代前一句城镇快速变成大城市的过程。
as 引导原因状语从句，说明挑战的具体来源。
suffered from 后接两个并列问题：pollution 和 inadequate sanitation。

sentences.sentence: Although industrialisation increased the country's economic output overall and improved the standard of living for the middle and upper classes, many poor people continued to struggle.
sentences.translation: 尽管工业化总体上提高了国家的经济产出，并改善了中上层阶级的生活水平，但许多穷人仍在艰难度日。

structure.text: Although industrialisation increased the country's economic output overall and improved the standard of living for the middle and upper classes
industrialisation
increased the country's economic output overall and improved the standard of living
many poor people continued to struggle

difficult_points: Although 引导让步状语从句，前后形成“总体改善”与“穷人仍困苦”的对比。
increased 和 improved 是从句中的并列谓语。
continued to struggle 强调穷人的困难在工业化后仍然持续。

sentences.sentence: Factory workers had to work long hours in dangerous conditions for extremely low wages.
sentences.translation: 工厂工人不得不在危险条件下长时间工作，却只能获得极低的工资。

structure.text: Factory workers
had to work long hours
in dangerous conditions
for extremely low wages

difficult_points: had to 表示被迫或必须，突出工人缺乏选择。
in dangerous conditions 说明工作环境，for extremely low wages 说明报酬。
long hours 是名词短语，表示“很长的工作时间”。

sentences.sentence: These conditions along with the rapid pace of change fuelled opposition to industrialisation.
sentences.translation: 这些状况连同快速的变化节奏一起激起了对工业化的反对。

structure.text: These conditions along with the rapid pace of change
along with the rapid pace of change
fuelled opposition to industrialisation

difficult_points: 主语核心是 These conditions，along with 补充另一个共同因素。
fuelled 是比喻用法，表示“助长、激起”。
opposition to industrialisation 表示“对工业化的反对”，to 后接反对对象。

sentences.sentence: A group of British workers who became known as "Luddites" were British weavers and textile workers who objected to the increased use of mechanised looms and knitting frames.
sentences.translation: 一群后来被称为“卢德派”的英国工人，是反对越来越多使用机械化织布机和针织机的英国织工和纺织工人。

structure.text: A group of British workers who became known as "Luddites"
who became known as "Luddites"
were British weavers and textile workers
who objected to the increased use of mechanised looms and knitting frames
objected to the increased use of mechanised looms and knitting frames

difficult_points: 第一个 who 修饰 British workers，说明他们后来被称为 Luddites。
第二个 who 修饰 British weavers and textile workers，说明他们反对什么。
objected to 后接名词短语 the increased use，to 不是不定式标记。

sentences.sentence: Many had spent years learning their craft, and they feared that unskilled machine operators were robbing them of their livelihood.
sentences.translation: 许多人花了多年时间学习自己的手艺，他们担心不熟练的机器操作员正在夺走他们的生计。

structure.text: Many had spent years learning their craft
learning their craft
and they feared
that unskilled machine operators were robbing them of their livelihood
were robbing them of their livelihood

difficult_points: Many 指代前文的织工和纺织工人。
had spent years learning 使用过去完成时，强调他们在反对之前已长期习艺。
that 引导 feared 的宾语从句，rob them of their livelihood 表示夺走他们的生计。

sentences.sentence: A few desperate weavers began breaking into factories and smashing textile machines.
sentences.translation: 少数绝望的织工开始闯入工厂并砸毁纺织机器。

structure.text: A few desperate weavers
began breaking into factories and smashing textile machines
breaking into factories and smashing textile machines

difficult_points: A few 表示数量不多，desperate 说明这些织工采取极端行动的心理状态。
began 后接两个并列动名词 breaking 和 smashing。
breaking into factories 表示非法闯入工厂，不是简单地“打破”。

sentences.sentence: They called themselves Luddites after Ned Ludd, a young apprentice who was rumoured to have wrecked a textile machine in 1779.
sentences.translation: 他们以奈德·卢德的名字称自己为卢德派；据传，这位年轻学徒曾在1779年毁坏过一台纺织机器。

structure.text: They
called themselves Luddites after Ned Ludd
a young apprentice
who was rumoured to have wrecked a textile machine in 1779
to have wrecked a textile machine in 1779

difficult_points: after Ned Ludd 表示“以奈德·卢德命名”，不是时间上的“在……之后”。
a young apprentice 是 Ned Ludd 的同位语。
was rumoured to have wrecked 使用被动加完成不定式，表示传闻中的过去行为。

sentences.sentence: The first major instances of machine breaking took place in 1811 in the city of Nottingham, and the practice soon spread across the country.
sentences.translation: 最早的重大破坏机器事件于1811年发生在诺丁汉市，这种做法很快蔓延到全国。

structure.text: The first major instances of machine breaking
took place in 1811 in the city of Nottingham
and the practice soon spread across the country
spread across the country

difficult_points: instances of machine breaking 指具体的破坏机器事件。
took place 表示“发生”，后面两个介词短语分别说明时间和地点。
the practice 指代 machine breaking 这种做法。

sentences.sentence: Machine-breaking Luddites attacked and burned factories, and in some cases they even exchanged gunfire with company guards and soldiers.
sentences.translation: 破坏机器的卢德派袭击并焚烧工厂，有时甚至与公司守卫和士兵交火。

structure.text: Machine-breaking Luddites
attacked and burned factories
and in some cases they even exchanged gunfire with company guards and soldiers
exchanged gunfire with company guards and soldiers

difficult_points: Machine-breaking 是复合形容词，修饰 Luddites，表示“破坏机器的”。
attacked and burned 是并列谓语，描述他们对工厂采取的行动。
in some cases 表示“在某些情况下”，降低后面交火行为的普遍性。

sentences.sentence: The workers wanted employers to stop installing new machinery, but the British government responded to the uprisings by making machine-breaking punishable by death.
sentences.translation: 工人们希望雇主停止安装新机器，但英国政府通过把破坏机器定为可判死刑的罪行来回应这些起义。

structure.text: The workers
wanted employers to stop installing new machinery
but the British government responded to the uprisings
by making machine-breaking punishable by death
making machine-breaking punishable by death

difficult_points: wanted employers to stop... 是“want + 宾语 + 不定式”结构。
but 引出工人与政府反应之间的强烈转折。
by making... 表示政府回应的方式，punishable by death 意为可判死刑。

sentences.sentence: The unrest finally reached its peak in April 1812, when a few Luddites were shot during an attack on a mill near Huddersfield.
sentences.translation: 这场动乱最终在1812年4月达到顶峰，当时几名卢德派在哈德斯菲尔德附近一次袭击磨坊的行动中被枪击。

structure.text: The unrest
finally reached its peak in April 1812
when a few Luddites were shot during an attack on a mill near Huddersfield
were shot during an attack on a mill near Huddersfield

difficult_points: reached its peak 表示事件发展到最高点或最激烈阶段。
when 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 April 1812 发生的具体事件。
were shot 是被动语态，during an attack 说明枪击发生的背景。

sentences.sentence: In the days that followed, other Luddites were arrested, and dozens were hanged or transported to Australia.
sentences.translation: 在随后的几天里，其他卢德派被逮捕，数十人被绞死或被流放到澳大利亚。

structure.text: In the days that followed
that followed
other Luddites were arrested
and dozens were hanged or transported to Australia
were hanged or transported to Australia

difficult_points: that followed 修饰 the days，表示“随后几天”。
were arrested、were hanged、transported 都是被动表达，突出他们受到的处罚。
dozens 指“数十人”，承接前面的 other Luddites。

sentences.sentence: By 1813, the Luddite resistance had all but vanished.
sentences.translation: 到1813年，卢德派的抵抗几乎已经消失。

structure.text: the Luddite resistance
had all but vanished

difficult_points: By 1813 与过去完成时 had vanished 搭配，表示到该时间点之前已经发生。
all but 表示“几乎”，不是“全部但是”的字面意思。
the Luddite resistance 指前文所述卢德派的集体抵抗活动。

sentences.sentence: * agrarian: relating to the land, especially the use of land for farming
sentences.translation: * agrarian：与土地有关，尤其指土地用于农业。

structure.text: * agrarian
relating to the land
especially the use of land for farming

difficult_points: 这是脚注释义，不是正文论证的一部分。
relating to the land 解释 agrarian 的基本含义。
especially 后进一步限定该词多与农业用地相关。
